title: 3 Backtesting Pitfalls That Ruin Your Trading Strategy
description:

*📈 Interactive Brokers for Algorithmic Trading*

*🪐 Jupyter Notebook*
https://github.com/romanmichaelpaolucci/Quant-Guild-Library/blob/main/2026%20Video%20Lectures/97.%203%20Backtesting%20Pitfalls%20That%20Ruin%20Your%20Strategy/qebp.ipynb

*📑 TL;DW Executive Summary*
*🕵️‍♂️ Pitfall 1: Look-Ahead Bias*
- The Error: Accidentally using future data in your backtest (e.g., referencing a bar's close price before the bar has actually closed).
- The Fix: Ensure your simulated trades and logic only use information available at time t. Your strategy must be adapted to the filtration.
*🎯 Pitfall 2: Overfitting & Data Snooping*
- The Error: Creating a strategy that looks legendary on historical data but collapses in live trading due to excessive complexity or curve-fitting.
- The Fix: Favor simple, robust models. Always validate your results using out-of-sample data and walk-forward analysis.
*💸 Pitfall 3: Ignoring Microstructure Costs*
- The Error: Neglecting transaction costs, spreads, or slippage. This can turn a "profitable" strategy into a guaranteed loser in the real world.
- The Fix: Always include realistic estimates for all trading frictions in every backtest you run.
*🏅 HONORABLE MENTIONS: THE ACCOMPLICES*
- Transaction Costs (TCs): Commissions and exchange fees incurred on every execution.
- Bid-Ask Spread: The cost of immediacy; the difference between where you can buy vs. where you can sell.
- Slippage: The gap between your expected price and the actual fill price, often caused by market impact or volatility.

I hope you enjoyed, and I hope you learned something!

- Roman
___________________________________________
